title: Karl-Marx-Allee: Quick Summary
items: **Karl-Marx-Allee is East Berlin's 2.3 km showpiece boulevard**, running dead straight from Alexanderplatz to Frankfurter Tor. It was built from 1952 as Stalinallee, and the wedding-cake blocks between Strausberger Platz and Frankfurter Tor are the stretch worth walking.

**Walk it east from U Strausberger Platz to Frankfurter Tor.** That is about 20 minutes non-stop, 45 with the details, and the U5 runs under the whole street, so you can bail out at Weberwiese or ride back from Frankfurter Tor at any point.

**The 1953 East German uprising started on this street.** On 16 June 1953 the construction workers building the 'workers' palaces' downed tools over raised work quotas; a day later Soviet tanks were crushing the protest. A memorial plaque on the Allee marks it.

**Kino International reopened in February 2026** after an 18 month renovation, so the GDR premiere cinema is showing films again, many in original language. Café Moskau opposite is a rented event venue, not a public café; Café Sibylle at number 72 is the real coffee stop.

**It pairs naturally with the free tour.** My free Berlin walking tour starts at Alexanderplatz, exactly where the boulevard begins, so the Allee is the cleanest add-on afterwards: one U5 stop to Strausberger Platz and you are on the 1950s stage set.
